item.title: Valve Explains Why It Doesn’t Subsidize Its Hardware Platforms 摘要
item.description:
Valve explains it does not sell its Steam Deck or Steam Machine at a loss because subsidizing hardware contradicts its belief in open ecosystems. The company argues that selling hardware below cost is a strategy to build closed systems, limiting consumer choice. Instead, Valve prefers to offer its devices as one option among many in the open PC platform, where customers can choose based on price, performance, and other factors. The article contrasts this with Sony and Microsoft's approach of selling consoles at a loss, and notes that mobile phone subsidies come from carriers, not manufacturers.
标签: Valve, Steam Deck, Steam Machine, hardware pricing, open ecosystem, gaming consoles, subsidies, PC gaming, business strategy, consumer choice

item.title: PlayStation Plus and Xbox Game Pass Subscriptions 摘要
item.description:
Valve objects to selling game consoles at a loss, unlike Sony and Microsoft which subsidize hardware costs through recurring subscriptions. PlayStation Plus ($11-$20/month) and Xbox Game Pass ($10-$23/month) are nearly mandatory for online multiplayer, costing more than the hardware within a few years. Valve's Steam Deck and Steam Machines, being PC-like, require only a free Steam account for online play, avoiding such hidden costs. The author argues Valve should have highlighted this advantage.
标签: Valve, PlayStation Plus, Xbox Game Pass, console hardware, subscription, online multiplayer, Steam Deck, Steam Machine, gaming, business model

item.title: The Winning Essays for the Big Questions About AI 摘要
item.description:
The article announces winners of an AI essay contest. First place: Jassi Pannu proposes using an OpenAI Foundation to end airborne transmission via far-UVC lamps and infrastructure, arguing it reduces both disease burden and pandemic risk. Second place: Ege Erdil advises countries outside the AI supply chain to focus on standard pro-growth policies (property rights, low taxes, open regulation) rather than radical schemes. Third place: Michael Li compares AI labs' business models to Hong Kong's MTR, suggesting they may profit from complementary assets rather than core products. The full winning essays are included.
标签: AI, biosecurity, pandemic preparedness, economic policy, infrastructure, public health, business model, technology policy, essay contest, OpenAI

item.title: ★ A Tale of Two Modems 摘要
item.description:
A Tata Electronics data breach reveals that the U.S. iPhone 18 Pro will use Qualcomm modems for mmWave support, while international models will use Apple's C2 modem, which lacks mmWave. The author argues that Apple's C-series modems offer better battery life and sufficient speeds, making mmWave unnecessary for most users. The decision to use Qualcomm in the U.S. is likely driven by carrier demands (Verizon, AT&T) for mmWave bragging rights, potentially resulting in worse battery life for U.S. models.
标签: iPhone 18 Pro, Apple, Qualcomm, modem, mmWave, 5G, battery life, carrier, Verizon, data breach
